qa_pairs.question: Is a RAC application eligible if I need 45 TB of project storage, split as 20 TB on Rorqual and 25 TB on Narval?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, this request is eligible because the total amount of project storage needed (45 TB) exceeds the 41 TB minimum.

qa_pairs.question: Can I submit a RAC application for a total of 100 TB of storage on Fir, comprised of 50 TB of project storage and 50 TB of nearline storage, even if the nearline amount is below the minimum?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, this request is eligible. Even though the nearline amount (50 TB) is below the minimum (101 TB), the project storage request (50 TB) is eligible. You should include both requests in your application.

qa_pairs.question: If I require 100 VCPU of compute cloud resources on Arbutus, along with 5 TB of Volume and snapshot storage and 5 TB of object storage, should I include all these resources in my online application form?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, you should request all these resources in the online form. Since the amount of VCPUs needed on Arbutus exceeds the minimum required to apply, the storage resources must be included in the same request, even if they are less than 11 TB.

qa_pairs.question: Will a RAC application be rejected if it only requests 250 core-years in total, split as 100 cores on Fir and 150 cores on Nibi?
qa_pairs.answer: Yes, this request will NOT be accepted because you may be able to get the CPU that you need without a RAC allocation. If your application only includes a CPU request in this way, it will be disqualified.

qa_pairs.question: Will a RAC application be accepted if I need 20 total GPU reference units (RGU), split as 10 RGU on Narval and 10 RGU on Rorqual?
qa_pairs.answer: No, this request will not be accepted. The minimum GPU request is 25 RGU-years in each cluster where resources are needed.

qa_pairs.question: Should I apply for RAC if I need 25 VCPU of persistent cloud resources on Arbutus with 10 TB of Volume and snapshot storage?
qa_pairs.answer: No, you should not apply for RAC for these resources, as they are available via the Rapid Access Service (RAS).

qa_pairs.question: What is a mandatory requirement regarding application document templates?
qa_pairs.answer: Applicants are required to use the latest version of the template provided for the corresponding application process. Failing to do so may result in the rejection of your application.

qa_pairs.question: What is the page limit for RAC applications, not including references?
qa_pairs.answer: Applications exceeding 10 pages (before References) will not be accepted.

qa_pairs.question: How should references be submitted with a RAC application?
qa_pairs.answer: References must be submitted as a separate document, unless the total number of pages of the entire application, including References, is 10 pages or less, in which case submitting everything in just one document is acceptable.

qa_pairs.question: What file format is required for submitting application documents?
qa_pairs.answer: Application documents must be submitted in PDF format.

qa_pairs.question: Where can applicants find the template for the Resources for Research Groups (RRG) application process?
qa_pairs.answer: Templates for Resources for Research Groups are available in Word or OpenDocument format (English and French) and LaTeX format (English and French) via the provided links.

qa_pairs.question: What template formats are available for the Research Platforms and Portals (RPP) application?
qa_pairs.answer: Templates for Research Platforms and Portals are available in Word or OpenDocument format (English and French) and LaTeX format (English and French) via the provided links.

qa_pairs.question: How can I download an editable version of the application template from a Google document link?
qa_pairs.answer: Once the non-editable Google document opens, go to `File → Download` in the main menu and select `Microsoft Word` or `OpenDocument Format` to download the template; then save it and start working in the downloaded document.
